name: Secret Essence, Inc.
description_short: Secret Essence, Inc. is a retail fragrance and natural wellness store in downtown Atlanta selling body oils, essential oils, incense, crystals, and beauty products.
description_long:
Secret Essence, Inc. is a brick-and-mortar retail store located at 103–105 Peachtree St SW in downtown Atlanta, Georgia. The business is owned and operated by Mohammad, who has built a reputation in the Atlanta area as a fragrance specialist. The store describes itself as the "Best Source of Fragrance in Atlanta, GA" and caters to walk-in customers Monday through Saturday from 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM. No founding year has been independently verified. The business maintains an online presence and shop at mysecretessence.com.

Secret Essence stocks an unusually wide range of fragrance and natural wellness products for a single retail location. Core offerings include body oils, perfume oils, essential oils, black soap, shea butter, incense, candles, and air fresheners. The store also carries spiritual and wellness-adjacent products such as sage, Palo Santo, Dragon Blood resin, and crystals. A notable specialty product is cold-press extracted Black Seed Oil, which the business emphasizes as a high-quality, minimally processed item. Oil burners and fragrance accessories round out the inventory.

What distinguishes Secret Essence is the depth of its fragrance selection — the store claims to carry Atlanta's largest collection of body oils, essential oils, and perfumes in one location. Owner Mohammad is described across customer reviews as knowledgeable and personally helpful in matching customers to scents. The store's Google rating of 5.0 out of 5 drawn from 1,065 reviews is exceptionally high and suggests a strong, loyal customer base. The "$" price tier indicates products are positioned as affordable and accessible.

Secret Essence is a well-regarded local retail shop with strong customer satisfaction scores and a distinctive product mix spanning mainstream fragrance and niche wellness items. Limitations include the lack of a verified BBB profile, unknown founding history, and no confirmed return or money-back policy. The store is closed on Sundays, and no mobile app exists. Importantly, this business has no connection to financial services, credit repair, or lending of any kind — its current categorization on finance directories is a data error.
pros: Exceptional Google rating of 5.0/5 from over 1,065 verified customer reviews
Claims Atlanta's largest single-location collection of body oils, essential oils, and perfumes
Owner Mohammad personally known for hands-on fragrance expertise and customer guidance
Carries specialty and hard-to-find products including cold-pressed Black Seed Oil, Palo Santo, and Dragon Blood resin
Budget-friendly price range ('$') making fragrance products accessible to a wide range of shoppers
Convenient downtown Atlanta location on Peachtree St SW with six-day-a-week hours
Online shop available at mysecretessence.com for customers outside the immediate area
cons: Closed on Sundays — limits access for weekend-only shoppers
No verified BBB profile or accreditation found
Return and money-back policy not publicly documented
Founding year and full business history are unverified
No mobile app; online shop functionality and inventory depth are unverified
best_for: Atlanta-area residents seeking a wide in-person fragrance and body oil selection
Natural wellness shoppers looking for black soap, shea butter, and cold-pressed oils
Spiritual and holistic wellness consumers interested in sage, crystals, incense, and Palo Santo
Budget-conscious buyers wanting quality fragrance products without department-store prices
diagnosis: Secret Essence, Inc. is best suited for fragrance enthusiasts and natural wellness shoppers in or visiting downtown Atlanta who want a wide, expert-curated selection of body oils, essential oils, and spiritual wellness products at accessible prices. The store's 5.0-star rating across more than a thousand reviews is a genuine signal of customer satisfaction. However, this business is a retail fragrance store with no connection to financial services, credit repair, or lending — its presence in a consumer finance directory is a miscategorization that should be corrected.
services: Palo Santo and Dragon Blood resin
